First-mover advantage is strong & 26/33 matchups flagged as first-mover dominance & Use score margins and depth metrics; avoid raw win-rate only (79\% flagged)\\
Alpha-Beta is stronger under equal time budgets & MM-simple-0.5s vs AB-simple-0.5s: median depth 8.0 vs 12.0 & Pruning buys deeper lookahead even when nodes/second is lower\\
Phase-aware evaluation has a heavy compute cost & AB-simple-2s vs AB-phaseaware-2s: nodes/s 636998 vs 106097, median depth 14.0 vs 11.0 & At low/medium time limits, simple eval often reaches deeper search\\
Phase-aware becomes competitive at deeper fixed-depth searches & AB-d13-simple vs AB-d13-phaseaware: median depth 13.0/13.0, margin -1.0 (A perspective) & Keep higher-depth phase-aware tests in the appendix to show this crossover\\
More time generally increases effective search depth & AB-simple-0.5s vs AB-simple-1s: median depth 12.0 -> 14.0 & Time-scaling curves are useful for choosing practical move-time budgets\\
